Chemical reactions involve transformations where new substances are formed. We can identify a chemical reaction by observing changes like a change in state, colour, evolution of a gas, or a change in temperature. Chemical reactions can be represented by word equations, listing reactants and products. For a more concise representation, chemical formulae are used to write chemical equations. An unbalanced chemical equation is a skeletal representation. A balanced chemical equation adheres to the law of conservation of mass, ensuring the number of atoms of each element is the same on both the reactant and product sides. This balancing is crucial as mass cannot be created or destroyed during a chemical reaction.
